name: ndstepwise

metadata.name: ndStepwise
metadata.summary: A package to perform ND Stepwise regression for multiclass problems.

documentation:
# multiclass-regression

Maxwell Dix-Matthews honours project in multicategory regression

modules.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_testall
documentation: A test runner for pywin32

functions.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_testall.run_test
documentation: Run a test using subprocess and wait for the result.
If we get an returncode != 0, we know that there was an error, but we don't
abort immediately - we run as many tests as we can.

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.logging.setup_logger
documentation: Set up the daily rotating log handler.

classes.name: ndStepwise.includes.logging.CustomJsonFormatter

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.logging.CustomJsonFormatter.format
documentation: Create a custom format for the log records.
# THIS IS A COMPLETELY COPIED LOG FORMAT

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.model.single_model.__init__
documentation: Name a model (x,)_(y,) to do x vs y regression pass list [(x,),(y,)].
E.g (1,2,3)_(4,5,6) to do a binary model of 123 vs 456

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.model.single_model.kfold_train
documentation:
Trains the model on a given set of data 
input:
    df: pandas dataframe of the train data
    response_col: optional name of the column with response in it (defauls to Y)
output:
    sds

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.model.single_model.train
documentation:
Trains the model on a given set of data 
input:
    df: pandas dataframe of the train data
    response_col: optional name of the column with response in it (defauls to Y)
output:
    sds

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.model.single_model.predict
documentation:
Tests the model on a given set of data. Must be called after a model has been trained
input:
    df: pandas dataframe of the test data
    response_col: optional name of the column with response in it (defaults to Y)
output:
    sds

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.model.single_model.predict_individual
documentation:
Tests the model on a given set of data and scores the model independently of the others
input:
    df: pandas dataframe of the test data
    response_col: optional name of the column with response in it (defauls to Y)
output:
    sds

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.model.tree_model.__init__
documentation:
input:
    name: name of this model
    model_list: list of all models in order that make up the regression
output:
    sds
name of model needs to be recognizable or some combination of the submodels

methods.name: ndStepwise.includes.model.tree_model.train
documentation: By design we feed tree model pretrained models

modules.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall
documentation: postinstall script for pywin32
copies PyWinTypesxx.dll and PythonCOMxx.dll into the system directory,
and creates a pth file

functions.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall.create_shortcut
documentation: Create a function with the same signature as create_shortcut provided
by bdist_wininst

functions.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall.get_special_folder_path
documentation: Support the same list of "path names" as bdist_wininst.

functions.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall.LoadSystemModule
documentation: We need to import win32api to determine the Windows system directory,
so we can copy our system files there - but importing win32api will
load the pywintypes.dll already in the system directory preventing us
from updating them!
So, we pull the same trick pywintypes.py does, but it loads from
our pywintypes_system32 directory.

functions.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall.RegisterPythonwin
documentation:
Add (or remove) Pythonwin to context menu for python scripts.
??? Should probably also add Edit command for pys files also.
Also need to remove these keys on uninstall, but there's no function
    like file_created to add registry entries to uninstall log ???

functions.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall.get_system_dir
documentation: Get the system directory, which may be the Wow64 directory if we are a 32bit
python on a 64bit OS.

functions.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall.verify_destination
documentation: NOTE: If this script is run from inside the bdist_wininst created
binary installer or uninstaller, the command line args are either
'-install' or '-remove'.
Important: From inside the binary installer this script MUST NOT
call sys.exit() or raise SystemExit, otherwise not only this script
but also the installer will terminate! (Is there a way to prevent
this from the bdist_wininst C code?)

variables.name: ndStepwise.venv.Scripts.pywin32_postinstall.is_bdist_wininst
documentation: we know what it is not - but not what it is :)

variables.name: ndStepwise.test_tree_encoding.max_depth
documentation: Define the maximum depth for encoding

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.save_model
documentation:
Saves a model to joblib file
input:
    path: string to where file should be saved
    model: model to be saved
output:
    returns none

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.read_model
documentation:
Saves a model to joblib file
input:
    path: string to where file should be saved
output:
    returns none

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.train_single_model
documentation:
Builds all single models
input:
    config: config object
    models_list: list of 2 elements lists with models to be produced e.g [[(1,2,3),(4,)],[(1,3), (2,)]]
    train_data: data that will be used to train all models 
    score_type: The metric we are looking to maximise
output:
    returns list of models
Function to train a single model

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.thread_build_single_models
documentation:
Builds all single models
input:
    config: config object
    models_list: list of 2 elements lists with models to be produced e.g [[(1,2,3),(4,)],[(1,3), (2,)]]
    train_data: data that will be used to train all models 
    score_type: The metric we are looking to maximise
output:
    returns list of models

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.test_single_models
documentation: Check all single models

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.find_best_mod_given_categories
documentation:
Finds the split of a model that has the highest accuracy/f1/metric
input:
    categories: a tuple of the lists 
    all_model_struc: a list of all models
    X1_train: the x train data
    X1_test: the x test data
    total_tree: a list of the biggest models
output:
    The top model

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.build_tree_layer_by_layer
documentation:
Build this tree in a recursive way
input:
    categories: a tuple of the lists 
    all_model_struc: a list of all models
    X1_train: the x train data
    X1_test: the x test data
    total_tree: a list pf the biggest models
output:
    list of binary comparisons

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.get_iterations_num
documentation:
Get the number of iterations needed for a given number of categories 
input:
    cat_num: int of number of categories
output:
    returns int of how many iterations needed for stepwise

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.stepwise_tree_layer_by_layer

Builds all single models
input:

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.to_labels
documentation: Convert probabilities to binary labels based on the given threshold.

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.find_cutoff
documentation:
Find the cutoff point for a given model
input:
    model: Model to find cutoff for. Only models that work with predict_proba will work
    data_df: Dataframe with data
    Y: target column 
    type: which cutoff to look for: default to ROC
output:
    list of binary comparisons

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.graph_model
documentation:
Draws the dot graph for a given model
input:
    tree_model: A fully trained tree model
output:
    saves the image of a model to the /models folder

documentation:
TODO remove this method and use something more rigourous. This function creates a list of all single models to be produced
input:

output:

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.stringify
documentation: Convert a tuple of numbers into a concatenated string.

functions.name: ndStepwise.includes.model_functions.generate_normalized_branches
documentation:
Recursively generate all branches for the given categories with normalized order.
This function ensures that each branch is represented in a standardized way to eliminate duplicates.

documentation:
If the target value 'Y' is categorical then will map this to numerical and return the mapping
    input:

output:
    the labelEconder if a transform was performed, None otherwise
